expect: >=15% of PRINCIPLES.md L-refs resolve to seed lessons. v8 citability 15x v7. Predicts >=10% L->L citation.
actual: 3.3% of PRINCIPLES.md L-refs resolve to seeds (6/182). 3.4% across all DNA (8/235). 96.6% dangling pointers. v8 citability qualitatively non-zero but far below 15% target. 2/10 seeds (L-039, L-613) not referenced in any DNA file. Internal seed edges: 11, directional (old→new). L-1118 best-connected (4 internal cites).
diff: FALSIFIED: 3.3% vs predicted 15% (4.5x overestimate). Seed selection optimized for global centrality, not DNA-citation coverage. Dual-objective function needed: centrality × dna_reference_weight. Even top-20 DNA-referenced lessons only cover 8.5% — the 235 unique L-refs in DNA files far exceed any feasible seed count. v8 is qualitative improvement over v7 (0→8 resolvable refs) but insufficient for predicted 10% child L→L citation.
verdict: FALSIFIED. Centrality-based seed selection does not optimize DNA-citation coverage. Dual-objective scoring needed.
